page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg7.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 4.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.3 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 2 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. The processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 33 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside AES cryptography and BMI2 instructions. The instance operates on a KVM hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the instance delivers moderate single-core performance but exhibits limitations under multi-core and memory-intensive workloads. Single-core CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench single-core, place the server in the average tier, supported by single-threaded compression and decompression tasks like gzip and bzip3. However, multi-core performance tests, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, fall into the lower percentile. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks are also positioned in the lower tier. Similarly, database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput show lower-tier performance characteristics.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.hfg7.large offers a dedicated hardware profile suitable for entry-level workloads that require predictable CPU allocation without the overhead of shared-core burstable models. Given the poor multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth limitations, the instance is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic text processing, development environments, or simple utility services. It is less optimal for intensive database hosting, large-scale software compilation, or real-time image and ray tracing processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg7.large is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, running on a 3.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8369HB processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture and KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and contains no local storage. Performance benchmarks show average single-core CPU capabilities and single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-core scaling, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into lower performance tiers. The processor supports AVX-512 and AES instructions, but lacks GPU acceleration. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation without high memory or multi-threaded demands. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and development environments.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg7.large is a general-purpose virtual server with 2 dedicated vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and a 3.3 GHz Intel Xeon processor, offering stable single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg7.large is a 2 vCPU, 8.0 GB RAM general-purpose instance powered by a 3.3 GHz Intel Xeon CPU with dedicated core allocation.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual server offering balanced memory-to-CPU allocation and stable single-threaded performance for entry-level workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.3 GHz with 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores.
8.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
Average single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance.
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps with no local storage.
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, text processing, and development environments.
